theory.intro: By taking a sample, recording it, and returning it before the next draw (**sampling with replacement**), you can estimate the makeup of a whole collection you cannot see.
facts: The **sample proportion** is $\dfrac{\text{number of that colour}}{\text{number of draws}}$.
Estimate of a colour in the whole $= \text{sample proportion} \times \text{total in collection}$.
Replacing each draw keeps the proportions the same for every draw.
Larger samples give estimates closer to the true makeup.
steps: Write the sample proportion for the colour as a fraction.
Multiply that proportion by the total size of the collection.
Round sensibly — the answer is an estimate, not exact.
